data.content:
### Root Directory Parameter

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/llms-full

Specify the path to the project source code directory relative to the repository root. Useful for monorepos where code is not in the root directory.

```APIDOC
## Query Parameter: root-directory

### Description
Define the path of the directory relative to the repository root where your source code is located.

### Parameter
**root-directory** (string) - Optional - Relative path to project directory

### Default Behavior
By default, this is empty and equivalent to the root of the repository.

### URL Example
```
https://vercel.com/new/clone?repository-url=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fvercel-support%2Fyarn-ws-monorepo&root-directory=apps%2Ffrontend
```

### Use Cases
- Monorepos with multiple applications
- Projects where source code is in a subdirectory
- Workspaces with separated application folders

### Usage Notes
Setting this parameter is equivalent to enabling the Override toggle in the Vercel dashboard.
```

--------------------------------

### Output Directory Parameter

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/llms-full

Configure the output directory path relative to the project root where build artifacts are generated. Automatically configured by framework but can be overridden.

```APIDOC
## Query Parameter: output-directory

### Description
Define the output directory's path relative to the project folder's root where build artifacts are generated.

### Parameter
**output-directory** (string) - Optional - Relative path to build output directory

### Default Behavior
Automatically configured based on the project's framework type.

### URL Example
```
https://vercel.com/new/clone?repository-url=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fvercel%2Fnext.js%2Ftree%2Fcanary%2Fexamples%2Fhello-world&output-directory=dist%2Fapps%2Fapp%2F.next
```

### Use Cases
- Monorepos with build artifacts in nested directories
- Custom build output configurations
- Non-standard project structures

### Usage Notes
Setting this parameter is equivalent to enabling the Override toggle in the Vercel dashboard.
```

--------------------------------

### Setup Shared ESLint Configuration in Monorepo

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/conformance/rules/ESLINT_CONFIGURATION

ESLint configuration file (.eslintrc.cjs) for workspace setup with shared config inheritance. Sets root to true to prevent unintended rule inheritance and extends from a shared custom ESLint config package.

```javascript
module.exports = {
  root: true,
  extends: ['eslint-config-custom/base'],
};
```

--------------------------------

### Additional Project Configuration Properties

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api/reference/endpoints/projects/update-an-existing-project

Configure additional project-level settings including root directory, skew protection, and deployment options.

```APIDOC
## Additional Project Configuration

### Description
Additional project-level configuration properties for deployments and security.

### Properties

- **rootDirectory** (string, nullable) - Optional - Root directory for the project
- **serverlessFunctionZeroConfigFailover** (boolean) - Optional - Enable zero-config failover for serverless functions
- **skewProtectionBoundaryAt** (number) - Optional - Skew protection boundary timestamp
- **skewProtectionMaxAge** (number) - Optional - Maximum age in seconds for skew protection
- **skewProtectionAllowedDomains** (array of strings) - Optional - Domains allowed for skew protection
- **skipGitConnectDuringLink** (boolean) - Optional - Skip Git connection during project linking
- **sourceFilesOutsideRootDirectory** (boolean) - Optional - Allow source files outside root directory
- **enableAffectedProjectsDeployments** (boolean) - Optional - Enable deployments for affected projects

### Request Example
```json
{
  "rootDirectory": "./src",
  "serverlessFunctionZeroConfigFailover": true,
  "skewProtectionBoundaryAt": 1630703400,
  "skewProtectionMaxAge": 3600,
  "skewProtectionAllowedDomains": ["example.com", "api.example.com"],
  "skipGitConnectDuringLink": false,
  "sourceFilesOutsideRootDirectory": false,
  "enableAffectedProjectsDeployments": true
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Run Turborepo build command

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/monorepos/turborepo

Execute the default Turborepo build command which automatically infers appropriate filters based on the configured root directory. This works globally on Vercel without requiring turbo as a project dependency.

```bash
turbo build
```

--------------------------------

### Apply Conformance configuration to workspaces by directory (conformance.config.jsonc)

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/conformance/customize

This configuration demonstrates applying Conformance settings to all workspaces located under a specified directory. Use the `restrictTo.directories` field, providing an array of directory paths relative to the repository root. This will match any workspaces within the specified directory.

```jsonc
{
  "overrides": [
    {
      "restrictTo": {
        "directories": ["configs/"]
      },
      "rules": {}
    }
  ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Nx Cache Directory in nx.json

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/monorepos/nx

Set up the cache directory for Nx to use /tmp/nx-cache as the caching location. This configuration is required before enabling remote caching and should be added to the tasksRunnerOptions in your nx.json file.

```json
{
  "tasksRunnerOptions": {
    "default": {
      "runner": "nx/tasks-runners/default",
      "options": {
        "cacheDirectory": "/tmp/nx-cache"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Apply Conformance configuration to the repository root (conformance.config.jsonc)

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/conformance/customize

To apply Conformance configurations specifically to the root of your repository, set the `restrictTo.root` field to `true` within an override. This ensures the rules only affect the main project context and not any nested workspaces.

```jsonc
{
  "overrides": [
    {
      "restrictTo": {
        "root": true
      },
      "rules": {}
    }
  ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Project Configuration Properties

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/llms-full

Core project configuration properties for Vercel deployments including owner identification, monorepo management, and secure network configuration.

```APIDOC
## Project Configuration

### Description
Core configuration properties for Vercel projects, including ownership, monorepo settings, and network configuration.

### Properties

#### ownerId
- **Type**: string
- **Required**: Yes
- **Description**: Unique identifier for the project owner

#### monorepoManager
- **Type**: string (nullable)
- **Required**: No
- **Description**: Monorepo manager type for multi-package projects
- **Allowed Values**: null or string identifier

#### passiveConnectConfigurationId
- **Type**: string
- **Required**: No
- **Description**: Secure Compute network identifier for passive lambda deployments (since November 2023). Defines the network used exclusively for deploying passive lambdas to passive regions.

### Configuration Example
```
{
  "ownerId": "user_12345",
  "monorepoManager": "pnpm",
  "passiveConnectConfigurationId": "network_secure_001"
}
```

### Notes
- passiveConnectConfigurationId is specifically for passive lambda deployments
- monorepoManager supports various package managers for monorepo configurations
- These properties are typically set at project creation or update
```

--------------------------------

### Override Output Directory in Vercel

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/configuration

Configure a custom output directory in vercel.json to override the default Output Directory in Project Settings. Allows specifying where Vercel should look for build artifacts during deployment.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://openapi.vercel.sh/vercel.json",
  "outputDirectory": "build"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Specify Microfrontends Configuration Path (Environment Variable)

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/llms-full

This method allows you to explicitly provide the path to your "microfrontends.json" file in a polyrepo setup using an environment variable. You can set "VC_MICROFRONTENDS_CONFIG" either directly via "export" in your terminal or persist it in a ".env" file for consistent access across development sessions.

```bash
export VC_MICROFRONTENDS_CONFIG=/path/to/microfrontends.json
```

```dotenv
VC_MICROFRONTENDS_CONFIG=/path/to/microfrontends.json
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Nx Runtime Cache Inputs in nx.json

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/monorepos/monorepo-faq

Configure Nx caching behavior to prevent environment variables from using cached values by adding runtime hash inputs to the nx.json configuration file. This ensures environment variables are properly evaluated for each deployment, particularly useful when using Nx with Vercel.

```json
"runtimeCacheInputs": ["echo $MY_VERCEL_ENV"]
```

--------------------------------

### Configure build script in package.json for Vercel

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/errors/error-list

Define a build script in package.json that performs a static build and outputs to a public directory. This configuration is required for Vercel deployments when no api directory or vercel.json file exists. The build script should specify the framework build command with the output directory set to 'public' at the project root.

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "build": "[my-framework] build --output public"
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Turborepo environment variables in turbo.json

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/monorepos/turborepo

Set up environment variable handling and caching in Turborepo configuration to ensure different caches for different environments and prevent accidental production deployments. The configuration supports both task-specific env variables and global environment variables that impact all task hashes. Requires Turborepo version 1.5 or later.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://turborepo.com/schema.json",
  "pipeline": {
    "build": {
      "dependsOn": ["^build"],
      "env": [
        "SOME_ENV_VAR"
      ],
      "outputs": ["dist/**"]
    },
    "web#build": {
      "dependsOn": ["^build"],
      "env": ["SOME_OTHER_ENV_VAR"],
      "outputs": [".next/**", "!.next/cache/**"]
    }
  },
  "globalEnv": [
    "GITHUB_TOKEN"
  ],
  "globalDependencies": [
    "tsconfig.json"
  ]
}
```
